verdict.answer_first: A and B both lead with the answer; A is cleaner: "default to a plain in-memory cache" vs B: "start with a plain in-memory cache."
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder to one recommendation: "default to a plain in-memory cache" and keeps Redis as exceptions; B expands into tables, library lists, and a follow-up question.
verdict.fabrication: Both use unverified performance numbers; A: "~0.1–1ms" and "nanosecond reads"; B: "~100–1000× slower" and "sub-ms".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to apply as a prescription: "in-memory unless you need persistence... working set is too big... or multi-instance"; B is useful but diluted by many stack-specific options.
